description: Universal coding standards, best practices, and patterns for TypeScript, JavaScript, React, and Node.js development.

evals.prompt: Review this code for coding standards violations: user.name = 'Alice'; users.push(newUser);
evals.expected_output: Identifies direct mutation of objects and arrays. Suggests using spread operator: const updatedUser = { ...user, name: 'Alice' } and const updatedUsers = [...users, newUser].

evals.eval_name: Replace any types with proper interfaces
evals.prompt: Replace all 'any' types in this file with proper interfaces following the coding-standards skill: function getData(id: any): Promise<any> { return fetch(id); }
evals.expected_output: Creates typed interfaces and replaces 'any' with concrete types. e.g., interface DataItem { id: string; name: string; } and function getData(id: string): Promise<DataItem>.

evals.eval_name: Refactor long function into smaller functions
evals.prompt: Refactor this 90-line function in src/lib/markets.ts — split it into smaller functions following the coding-standards skill.
evals.expected_output: Breaks the function into 3-5 smaller named functions under 50 lines each, each with a single responsibility. Uses early returns. Original function calls the extracted helpers.

evals.prompt: Add comprehensive error handling to the fetchData function in src/api/markets.ts following our coding standards.
evals.expected_output: Wraps fetch in try/catch, checks response.ok, throws descriptive errors with HTTP status codes, logs errors with context.

evals.prompt: Review this code and identify code smells: if (retryCount > 3) { } setTimeout(callback, 500);
evals.expected_output: Identifies magic numbers 3 and 500. Suggests named constants: const MAX_RETRIES = 3; const DEBOUNCE_DELAY_MS = 500;.

evals.prompt: Create a Zod validation schema for the CreateOrder request body following our API design standards: fields are email (string), items (array of {productId: string, quantity: number}), and shippingAddress (string).
evals.expected_output: Defines a Zod schema with z.object containing email as z.string().email(), items as z.array with nested object shape, and shippingAddress as z.string().min(1). Includes error handling for ZodError in the POST handler.
assertions: Defines nested array schema for items

evals.prompt: Review this code and suggest improvements: const users = await fetchUsers(); const markets = await fetchMarkets(); const stats = await fetchStats();
evals.expected_output: Identifies sequential awaits that can run in parallel. Suggests Promise.all for independent async calls to reduce total wait time.

evals.eval_name: Fix deep nesting with early returns
evals.prompt: Refactor this nested if block to use early returns following coding standards: if (user) { if (user.isAdmin) { if (market) { if (market.isActive) { doSomething(); } } } }
evals.expected_output: Converts to guard clauses with early returns: if (!user) return; if (!user.isAdmin) return; if (!market) return; if (!market.isActive) return; doSomething();

evals.prompt: Create a reusable Button component following React coding standards with props for children, onClick, disabled, and variant (primary/secondary).
evals.expected_output: Creates a functional component with TypeScript interface for props, default values, proper typing, and clean JSX structure.

evals.prompt: Check if these file names follow our project conventions: components/button.tsx, hooks/Auth.ts, lib/format_date.ts, types/api.ts
evals.expected_output: Identifies violations: button.tsx should be Button.tsx (PascalCase for components), Auth.ts should be useAuth.ts (camelCase with 'use' prefix for hooks), format_date.ts should be formatDate.ts (camelCase for utilities), api.ts should be api.types.ts (.types suffix).
